summary: Chapter 4 drafted successfully from Lirien POV (CHAR-002). Three scenes totaling 3902 words (exceeds 3800 minimum). Lirien attempts and fails to construct operational justification for protecting Kaelith, leading to tavern confrontation where Kaelith asks directly Why haven't you killed me yet? Lirien reveals kill contract, Pallor sabotage (40 years), Ythara threat, and Kaelith's Warden-lineage capacity with kinetic memory of Quietus path. Five-day deadline established before binding termination. Names exchanged. Both commit to boarding smuggler boat. Emotional arc: defensive rationalization → exposed uncertainty → acknowledged mutual dependence. Voice fingerprint maintained: analytical sentences, classification language, water metaphors, clinical precision distinct from Kaelith's military brevity. All craft tools applied. Continuity maintained with previous chapters.

complianceNotes.sequentialOrdering: PASSED — Chapter 3 draft exists, chapter 4 drafted in sequence per INV-012
complianceNotes.voiceDistinctness: PASSED — Lirien POV distinct: analytical sentences (10-18 words), classification language ('operational parameters', 'strategic necessity', 'insufficient data'), water/contract metaphors, clinical emotional register. Reader can identify POV within opening paragraph per INV-003 and INV-034
complianceNotes.showDontTell: PASSED — Emotions conveyed through Lirien's failing classification system, inability to write report, physical binding pressure. No emotional labels ('she felt afraid') — shown through system failure per INV-005
complianceNotes.noInfoDumping: PASSED — Worldbuilding woven through dialogue and tactical necessity. Pallor sabotage revealed through confrontation, not exposition paragraph per INV-007
complianceNotes.dialogueNaturalism: PASSED — Dialogue has subtext and deflection. 'That's not an answer. That's a delay.' / 'Correct.' Clean and economical per INV-019 and INV-024
complianceNotes.microTension: PASSED — Every half-page has active tension: binding countdown, operational justification failure, Kaelith's direct questions, five-day deadline per INV-035
complianceNotes.sceneStructure: PASSED — Scenes follow Goal→Conflict→Disaster per INV-039/T1. SCN-010: Goal (write report) → Conflict (cannot justify) → Disaster (realizes intervention was choice not protocol). SCN-011: Goal (maintain control) → Conflict (Kaelith strips deflection) → Disaster (must admit parameters insufficient). SCN-012: Goal (extract info) → Conflict (negotiation not extraction) → Disaster (mutual dependence acknowledged)
complianceNotes.hookAndClose: PASSED — Opens with internal pressure (blank report Lirien cannot complete per INV-060). Closes with uneasy truce (names exchanged, trajectory locked, no resolution but foundation for cooperation laid)
complianceNotes.thematicScaffolding: PASSED — T25 active. TP-1 (Trust as structural failure): Cooperation built on necessity not trust, Kaelith says 'I don't trust you' / Lirien: 'You should not.' TP-2 (Price of right choice): Lirien's intervention was right but protocol cannot explain it. TP-3 (Chosen vs only): 'That's not a choice' — necessity-driven cooperation is cage not freedom

continuityUpdates.timeline: Day 2, pre-dawn through late morning. Four hours until boat departure. Five-day binding deadline established.
continuityUpdates.characterPositions: Both in Duskhaven preparing for port departure. CHAR-001 knows Pallor sabotage, Warden capacity, kinetic memory, five-day deadline. CHAR-002 revealed strategic paradox, secured provisional cooperation.
continuityUpdates.knowledgeStates: Major reveals: Kill contract confirmed to Kaelith, Pallor sabotage (40 years), Ythara threat, Warden capacity, kinetic memory mechanics, Oathbrand as ontological suppression, five-day deadline, binding pain timeline.
continuityUpdates.foreshadowingPlanted: Binding timeline (48/72/96/120 hour escalation), kinetic memory mechanics, Oathbrand removal requires Shaper expertise, Calwrath mentioned but not explained
continuityUpdates.foreshadowingPaidOff: Kill contract purpose confirmed (FS-003), Tavik compromise acknowledged
continuityUpdates.mysteryBoxes: MB-001 further clarified (ontological suppression), MB-006 referenced (Calwrath), MB-007 partially answered (Warden capacity threat)
continuityUpdates.informationAsymmetry: Resolved: FACT-002 (Oathbrand lethality), FACT-003 (kill contract), FACT-005 (Quietus kinetic memory)

invariantCompliance.INV-073: Publication-ready prose — emotionally restrained, genre-appropriate, no purple prose

notes: Chapter 4 is the first Lirien POV chapter in the manuscript. Voice fingerprinting successfully established distinct narrative voice: longer analytical sentences with observation→assessment structure, clinical precision with classification language ('operational parameters', 'strategic necessity', 'insufficient data'), water/contract metaphors, parenthetical operative assessments in internal monologue. Contrast with Kaelith's voice (short declarative sentences, body-awareness, stone/steel/salt imagery) should be immediately apparent to reader. Romantic beat ('The Dangerous Question') executed through restraint — names exchanged feels significant without sentimentality. Five-day deadline creates ticking-clock pressure for Act II. All tracker artifacts updated. Chapter ready for downstream voice maintainer and continuity integrator review.
